data.description:
Sweep (rebar-debug, sibling of 0e15) found two non-CLI bug-close call sites that never got the ed13 --class requirement, so both now fail to close BUG tickets:

1. src/rebar/_mcp_writes.py:117 — MCP tool transition_ticket() calls rebar.transition(id, current, target) with NO close_class. The library rebar.transition/_lib_writes.transition (line 337) DOES support close_class=. transition_core (txn.py:253) enforces --class unconditionally on any *->closed of a bug (unless current=='idea'), and force/force_close do NOT bypass it. => an agent over MCP CANNOT close any bug ticket (raises 'requires --class'). Regression: MCP could close bugs before ed13. (This is NOT the deliberate no-force policy; close_class is a required classification, not a bypass.)

2. src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py:356 — replay closes via transition_compute(local,'open','closed',force=True) with no close_class; force!=force_close and neither bypasses the class guard. Re-materializing a closed BUG from an NDJSON dump is rejected and the per-row fail-open (line 357) leaves the bug OPEN with a warning => import cannot restore closed bugs.

FIX: thread close_class through both. MCP: add a close_class parameter to transition_ticket and pass to rebar.transition (only needed for bug close; empty ok for non-bug). Import: carry the source close event's close_class if present, else default (e.g. 'undetermined'/'duplicate'). RED->GREEN each: MCP tool closes a bug with a valid class; import re-materializes a closed bug. CLI create/close and the canary (Gerrit 853) are handled; probe-rebar closes non-bugs (exempt).
data.title: ed13 --class requirement not propagated to MCP transition_ticket + NDJSON import bug-close paths
